author: Dr. Daniel Okafor / Dr. Marisol Vega
content:
MEDICINE — DAILY PROGRESS NOTE
Hospital day 3.

SUBJECTIVE: Chest pain improved; breathing easier; ambulating in the halls. No bleeding, no new symptoms.

OBJECTIVE:
- Hemodynamically stable, off supplemental oxygen (SpO2 96% RA). On therapeutic heparin infusion.
- Liver MRI (IMG004) resulted: a 17 cm posterior-right-lobe abnormality, additional left-lobe foci, and apparent thrombus in branches of the right portal vein; radiology with HIGH SUSPICION for malignancy and recommends considering needle biopsy.
- LFTs mildly abnormal with preserved synthetic function; tumor markers nonspecific (AFP mildly elevated; CEA/CA19-9 normal); hepatitis B/C negative.

ASSESSMENT/PLAN:
#Acute VTE — continue therapeutic anticoagulation; clinically improving.
#Liver mass concerning for malignancy — tissue diagnosis recommended. Multidisciplinary discussion underway (hematology, interventional radiology, hepatology) regarding the biopsy and anticoagulation plan. Triple-phase liver CT ordered for further characterization.
#Anticipate transition to oral anticoagulation and outpatient completion of the workup if he remains stable.
(Timing specifics deferred to multidisciplinary planning.)
attestation: Electronically signed by Dr. Daniel Okafor, MD (PGY-2) and Dr. Marisol Vega, MD. 7/8/2027.
